metadata.description: Centralized marketplace for sharing Claude Code plugins, instructions, and configurations across teams

plugins.name: coding-standards
plugins.description: Shared coding conventions for TypeScript, .NET, Python, plus git workflow, testing, architecture, and AI steering rules

plugins.name: writing-style
plugins.description: Tone of voice, writing style, AI tell avoidance, and communication guidelines

plugins.name: security-compliance
plugins.description: Security baseline rules and audit skills for maintaining secure code

plugins.name: thinking
plugins.description: Structured thinking and reasoning skills — first principles, ISC, algorithm, council, red team, and more

plugins.name: dotnet-stack
plugins.description: Framework-specific conventions for JasperFx (Wolverine/Marten) on .NET

plugins.name: nextjs-stack
plugins.description: Framework-specific conventions for Next.js, React, and Vercel

plugins.name: python-stack
plugins.description: Framework-specific conventions for Python projects — Django, python-eventsourcing, and related tooling

plugins.name: plugin-curator
plugins.description: Plugin curator — creates, reviews, and maintains agents, skills, and rules. Enforces structural consistency.

plugins.name: tooling
plugins.description: Organisational tooling conventions — which tool for which function. Ensures agents reference the correct tools.

plugins.name: publishing
plugins.description: Brand-styled markdown-to-PDF rendering — A4 documents with hps.gd typography and palette, optional cover page from frontmatter, swappable stylesheets.

plugins.name: coordinator
plugins.description: CEO/founder proxy — cross-team coordination, OKRs, initiative decomposition, strategic decisions spanning CPO and CTO

plugins.name: cpo
plugins.description: Chief Product Officer — coordinates product, design, content, GTM, and support teams

plugins.name: cto
plugins.description: Chief Technology Officer — coordinates architecture, development, QA, DevOps, security, and data engineering

plugins.name: grc-lead
plugins.description: GRC Lead — governance, risk management, regulatory compliance, AI governance, audit readiness

plugins.name: product-owner
plugins.description: Product owner — requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, backlog prioritisation

plugins.name: ui-designer
plugins.description: UI designer — visual design, design system, component specifications, accessibility

plugins.name: ux-researcher
plugins.description: UX researcher — customer journeys, touchpoints, information architecture, usability assessment, personas

plugins.name: user-docs-writer
plugins.description: User documentation writer — guides, tutorials, knowledge base, onboarding. Product language for non-technical audiences.

plugins.name: developer-docs-writer
plugins.description: Developer documentation writer — API references, SDK guides, integration tutorials, code examples.

plugins.name: internal-docs-writer
plugins.description: Internal documentation writer — architecture docs, runbooks, changelogs, onboarding, post-mortems.

plugins.name: gtm
plugins.description: Go-to-market specialist — positioning, launch strategy, content marketing, competitive analysis

plugins.name: support
plugins.description: Customer support — ticket triage, feedback synthesis, knowledge base, bug escalation

plugins.name: customer-success
plugins.description: Customer Success Manager — health monitoring, churn prevention, expansion, onboarding quality

plugins.name: engineering-conventions
plugins.description: Shared rules for engineering plugins: spec-first implementation. Installed automatically as a dependency of ai-engineer, data-engineer, devops, python-developer, qa-engineer, react-developer, and security-engineer

plugins.name: architect
plugins.description: Software architect — system design, ADRs, technology evaluation, API strategy

plugins.name: react-developer
plugins.description: React/Next.js developer — TypeScript, Tailwind, content-collections, react-pdf, Vitest

plugins.name: dotnet-developer
plugins.description: .NET/C# developer — Wolverine, Marten, event sourcing, CQRS, Alba testing

plugins.name: python-developer
plugins.description: Python developer — strict typing, Ruff, mypy, BDD, Hypothesis, DDD

plugins.name: ai-engineer
plugins.description: AI/ML engineer — prompt engineering, model evaluation, RAG pipelines, embeddings, AI feature implementation

plugins.name: qa-lead
plugins.description: QA Lead — test strategy, acceptance criteria, 3 amigos, edge case identification

plugins.name: qa-engineer
plugins.description: QA engineer — test automation, test execution, coverage analysis, bug investigation

plugins.name: release-manager
plugins.description: Release manager — release coordination, deployment scheduling, rollback decisions, go/no-go gates

plugins.name: performance-engineer
plugins.description: Performance engineer — load testing, profiling, capacity planning, performance budgets

plugins.name: devops
plugins.description: DevOps / platform engineer — IaC, CI/CD, deployment, monitoring, incident response

plugins.name: security-engineer
plugins.description: Security engineer — threat modelling, security audits, compliance, vulnerability management

plugins.name: data-engineer
plugins.description: Data engineer — data pipelines, analytics, event tracking, metrics, data modelling

plugins.name: code-reviewer
plugins.description: Code review agent with multi-pass review, quality scoring, and PR creation skills

plugins.name: research-conventions
plugins.description: Shared rules for research output: report frontmatter contract, file naming, source citation quality. Installed automatically as a dependency of analyst, investigator, and dossier

plugins.name: analyst
plugins.description: Research and business intelligence agents — web research with source attribution, and company/market analysis from public sources

plugins.name: investigator
plugins.description: OSINT investigation agents — domain and infrastructure intel, and people/entity investigation with mandatory ethical authorisation gates

plugins.name: dossier
plugins.description: Consolidate research outputs that conform to the report frontmatter contract into a single brand-styled PDF dossier, or drive an end-to-end campaign on a target

plugins.name: workflow-tools
plugins.description: Utility skills for common engineering workflow patterns — content retrieval with four-tier escalation strategy (WebFetch → curl → Playwright → BrightData)

plugins.name: billing-engineer
plugins.description: Billing engineer — subscription billing logic, invoicing, payment gateway integration, dunning management, and revenue recognition workflows.
